result:
Done — all three missing operations are implemented in `Spans.cs` and the project builds cleanly with no warnings (1 of 10 build cycles used).

- `ContainsPoint` scans the intervals and returns true when `starts[i] <= x < ends[i]`, honoring the half-open semantics.
- `ClipCovered` sums `min(ends[i], hi) - max(starts[i], lo)` per interval, counting only positive overlaps, so partial overlaps contribute their clipped portion and disjoint intervals contribute nothing.
- `GapCount` counts consecutive pairs with `starts[i+1] - ends[i] > 0`, so touching intervals form no gap and zero or one interval yields 0.

`TotalCovered` is untouched, and the new methods follow its existing style (static methods, plain `for` loops over the parallel arrays, pure integer arithmetic).
